yesterdayAnalysis: Yesterday delivered the planned long run (11mi, TSS 119, HR 149) plus open water swim (1.1mi, TSS 39), hitting the week's 10-11mi run target. However, the run HR of 149 is elevated for long aerobic work, and combined with Thursday's back-to-back 11mi runs (May 17 and May 22), you've now logged 22 miles of long runs in 6 days without adequate recovery between efforts. This violates the principle of hard-easy cycling and increases injury risk, especially with Perthes considerations.
todayRecommendation: Today's 17.9mi bike logged zero TSS, indicating either a recovery spin or data recording issue. With 61 TSS remaining to hit your 420 target and one day left in the week, prioritize active recovery today—easy 30-40min spin or complete rest. DO NOT attempt to chase the TSS gap with intensity; you're at 85% of target with adequate volume completed.
weekProgress: Week TSS at 359/420 (85%) is solid, but discipline distribution is concerning: only 107 bike TSS versus 284 run/swim TSS creates imbalance. The week called for your first 45-50mi outdoor ride—today's 17.9mi falls short of that benchmark. You've completed the long run target excellently (11mi) but missed the signature long bike session that defines this week's focus.
keyInsight: You're running yourself into fatigue with back-to-back long runs (11mi on 5/17, 11mi on 5/22) while underdelivering on bike volume—this inverts your race-day priority where conservative cycling enables the run PR.
